[[File:Sakatokejinja01n4592.jpg|thumb|240px|{{ill|Sanjagu|ja|三社宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanjagu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]]
[[File:Sakatokejinja04 1024.jpg|thumb|240px|{{ill|Mikoshi Storehouse|ja|神輿庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi Storehouse|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]]

'''Sakatoke Shrine''' (自玉手祭来酒解神社, たまでよりまつりきたるさかとけじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine}} located in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Otokuni District, Kyoto|en|Otokuni District, Kyoto|WD=Q376858|lt=Otokun District}}, {{ill|Ōyamazaki, Kyoto|en|Ōyamazaki, Kyoto|WD=Q450287|lt=Ōyamazaki Town}}. Its common name is '''Sakatoke Shrine''' (酒解神社, さかとけじんじゃ, さかときじんじゃ). It is situated near the summit of {{ill|Mount Tennōzan|en|Mount Tennōzan|WD=Q10940452|lt=Tennozan}}. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai Shrine|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|lt=Meishindai|lt_ja=名神大|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and in the old {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was designated as a Gōsha.

==Saijin==
It enshrines {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} as its main deity and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo}} as its secondary deity.

==History==
The origins of its founding are unclear; however, a ridge plaque dating to the first year of the {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō}} era ({{ill|717|en|717|WD=Q23532|lt=717 AD}}) indicates that it was established during the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}. It was formerly known as Yamazaki no Mori (山埼杜) and was enshrined at the site of the present {{ill|Rikyū Hachiman Shrine|ja|離宮八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rikyū Hachiman Shrine|1=Rikyū Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659484}}. In the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, the {{ill|Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} recorded the name as “Yamashiro Province, Otokun District, Sakatoke Shrine, formerly Yamazaki no Mori,” listing it among the state-sponsored {{ill|Meishindai Shrine|ja|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meishindai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} and noting that offerings for the monthly and New Rice Festival were entrusted to it.

Subsequently, the enshrinement at Sakatoke Shrine lapsed, and its whereabouts were unknown until the Meiji period. The present Sakatoke Shrine was formerly the Yamazaki Tenno Shrine, which enshrined the deity Tenno Hachijōjin ({{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō}}) that had been venerated near the summit of Tennozan since medieval times. Although Tennozan was originally called Yamazaki-yama, it came to be known as Tennozan in association with this shrine. In June of the 10th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, the Yamazaki Tenno Shrine was recognized as a Shikinai Shrine and renamed Sakatoke Shrine. The current main deity, Ōyamatsumi, was designated at that time.

The shrine’s mikoshi storehouse has been designated as an important cultural property by the nation.

== cultural property ==
* Honden – Registered Tangible Cultural Property of the nation ({{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}})
* Mikoshi storehouse – Important Cultural Property of the nation; an Itakura structure from the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}
* Mikoshi (2 units) – Works dating from before the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}}

== Subsidiary shrines ==
* Guardian Shrine: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}}
* Palace Master Shrine: {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|en|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|WD=Q643763|lt=Ashinazuchi, Tenazuchi}}
* Three Shrines ({{ill|Sanjagu|ja|三社宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanjagu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}):
** {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} 
** {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|en|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi, Great Deity}}
** {{ill|Hiruko Shrine|ja|Hiruko|lt=Hiruko|lt_ja=蛭子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Itsukushima Shrine: {{ill|Ichikishima-hime Shrine|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt_ja=市杵嶌姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}

== Events ==
* {{ill|Annual Festival|ja|例祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Annual Festival|1=reisai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385469}} – May 3 to 5 (the mikoshi procession takes place on May 5 every other year)
* New Year’s Festival – December 31

* {{ill|Myōki-an|ja|妙喜庵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōki-an|1=Myōki-an|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11446865}}
** {{ill|Taian|ja|待庵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taian|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 妙喜庵|1=Myōki-an|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11446865}}
* {{ill|Ōyamazaki Sansō Art Museum|ja|大山崎山荘美術館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazaki Sansō Art Museum|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アサヒグループ大山崎山荘美術館|1=Asahi Group Oyamazaki Villa Museum of Art|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11283175}}
* {{ill|Ōyamazaki Town Historical Museum|ja|大山崎町歴史資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazaki Town Historical Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Dainen-ji Temple|ja|大念寺 (京都府大山崎町)|lt=Dainen-ji|lt_ja=大念寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainen-ji Temple|1=Dainen-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435456}}

'''Sakatoke Shrine''' (自玉手祭来酒解神社, たまでよりまつりきたるさかとけじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine}} located in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Otokuni District, Kyoto|en|Otokuni District, Kyoto|WD=Q376858|lt=Otokun District}}, {{ill|Ōyamazaki, Kyoto|en|Ōyamazaki, Kyoto|WD=Q450287|lt=Ōyamazaki Town}}. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai Shrine|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|lt=Meishindai|lt_ja=名神大|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and in the old {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was designated as a Gōsha.

It enshrines {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} as its main deity and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo}} as its secondary deity.

==History==
The origins of its founding are unclear; however, a ridge plaque dating to the first year of the {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō}} era ({{ill|717|en|717|WD=Q23532|lt=717 AD}}) indicates that it was established during the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}. Subsequently, the enshrinement at Sakatoke Shrine lapsed, and its whereabouts were unknown until the Meiji period. The present Sakatoke Shrine was formerly the Yamazaki Tenno Shrine, which enshrined the deity Tenno Hachijōjin ({{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō}}) that had been venerated near the summit of Tennozan since medieval times. Although Tennozan was originally called Yamazaki-yama, it came to be known as Tennozan in association with this shrine. In June of the 10th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, the Yamazaki Tenno Shrine was recognized as a Shikinai Shrine and renamed Sakatoke Shrine. The current main deity, Ōyamatsumi, was designated at that time.

The shrine’s mikoshi storehouse has been designated as an important cultural property by the nation.

== cultural property ==
* Honden – Registered Tangible Cultural Property of the nation ({{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}})

== Subsidiary shrines ==
* Guardian Shrine: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}}

[[File:Sakatokejinja01n4592.jpg|thumb|240px|{{ill|Sanjagu|ja|三社宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanjagu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]]
[[File:Sakatokejinja04 1024.jpg|thumb|240px|{{ill|Mikoshi Storehouse|ja|神輿庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi Storehouse|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]]

'''Sakatoke Shrine''' (自玉手祭来酒解神社, たまでよりまつりきたるさかとけじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine}} located in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Otokuni District, Kyoto|en|Otokuni District, Kyoto|WD=Q376858|lt=Otokun District}}, {{ill|Ōyamazaki, Kyoto|en|Ōyamazaki, Kyoto|WD=Q450287|lt=Ōyamazaki Town}}. Its common name is '''Sakatoke Shrine''' (酒解神社, さかとけじんじゃ, さかときじんじゃ). It is situated near the summit of {{ill|Mount Tennōzan|en|Mount Tennōzan|WD=Q10940452|lt=Tennozan}}. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai Shrine|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|lt=Meishindai|lt_ja=名神大|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and in the old {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was designated as a Gōsha.

==Saijin==
It enshrines {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} as its main deity and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo}} as its secondary deity.

==History==
The origins of its founding are unclear; however, a ridge plaque dating to the first year of the {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō}} era ({{ill|717|en|717|WD=Q23532|lt=717 AD}}) indicates that it was established during the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}. It was formerly known as Yamazaki no Mori (山埼杜) and was enshrined at the site of the present {{ill|Rikyū Hachiman Shrine|ja|離宮八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rikyū Hachiman Shrine|1=Rikyū Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659484}}. In the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, the {{ill|Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} recorded the name as “Yamashiro Province, Otokun District, Sakatoke Shrine, formerly Yamazaki no Mori,” listing it among the state-sponsored {{ill|Meishindai Shrine|ja|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meishindai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} and noting that offerings for the monthly and New Rice Festival were entrusted to it.

Subsequently, the enshrinement at Sakatoke Shrine lapsed, and its whereabouts were unknown until the Meiji period. The present Sakatoke Shrine was formerly the Yamazaki Tenno Shrine, which enshrined the deity Tenno Hachijōjin ({{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō}}) that had been venerated near the summit of Tennozan since medieval times. Although Tennozan was originally called Yamazaki-yama, it came to be known as Tennozan in association with this shrine. In June of the 10th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, the Yamazaki Tenno Shrine was recognized as a Shikinai Shrine and renamed Sakatoke Shrine. The current main deity, Ōyamatsumi, was designated at that time.

The shrine’s mikoshi storehouse has been designated as an important cultural property by the nation.

== cultural property ==
* Honden – Registered Tangible Cultural Property of the nation ({{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}})
* Mikoshi storehouse – Important Cultural Property of the nation; an Itakura structure from the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}
* Mikoshi (2 units) – Works dating from before the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}}

== Subsidiary shrines ==
* Guardian Shrine: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}}

* Three Shrines ({{ill|Sanjagu|ja|三社宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanjagu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}):
** {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} 
** {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|en|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi, Great Deity}}
** {{ill|Hiruko Shrine|ja|Hiruko|lt=Hiruko|lt_ja=蛭子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Itsukushima Shrine: {{ill|Ichikishima-hime Shrine|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt_ja=市杵嶌姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}

It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai Shrine|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|lt=Meishindai|lt_ja=名神大|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and in the old {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was designated as a Gōsha.

==Saijin==
It enshrines {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} as its main deity and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo}} as its secondary deity.

==History==
The origins of its founding are unclear; however, a ridge plaque dating to the first year of the {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō}} era ({{ill|717|en|717|WD=Q23532|lt=717 AD}}) indicates that it was established during the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}. It was formerly known as Yamazaki no Mori (山埼杜) and was enshrined at the site of the present {{ill|Rikyū Hachiman Shrine|ja|離宮八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rikyū Hachiman Shrine|1=Rikyū Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659484}}. In the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, the {{ill|Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} recorded the name as “Yamashiro Province, Otokun District, Sakatoke Shrine, formerly Yamazaki no Mori,” listing it among the state-sponsored {{ill|Meishindai Shrine|ja|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meishindai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} and noting that offerings for the monthly and New Rice Festival were entrusted to it.

Subsequently, the enshrinement at Sakatoke Shrine lapsed, and its whereabouts were unknown until the Meiji period. The present Sakatoke Shrine was formerly the Yamazaki Tenno Shrine, which enshrined the deity Tenno Hachijōjin ({{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō}}) that had been venerated near the summit of Tennozan since medieval times. Although Tennozan was originally called Yamazaki-yama, it came to be known as Tennozan in association with this shrine. In June of the 10th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, the Yamazaki Tenno Shrine was recognized as a Shikinai Shrine and renamed Sakatoke Shrine. The current main deity, Ōyamatsumi, was designated at that time.

The shrine’s mikoshi storehouse has been designated as an important cultural property by the nation.

== cultural property ==
* Honden – Registered Tangible Cultural Property of the nation ({{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}})
* Mikoshi storehouse – Important Cultural Property of the nation; an Itakura structure from the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}
* Mikoshi (2 units) – Works dating from before the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}}

== Subsidiary shrines ==
* Guardian Shrine: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}}
* Palace Master Shrine: {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|en|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|WD=Q643763|lt=Ashinazuchi, Tenazuchi}}
* Three Shrines ({{ill|Sanjagu|ja|三社宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanjagu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}):
** {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} 
** {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|en|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi, Great Deity}}
** {{ill|Hiruko Shrine|ja|Hiruko|lt=Hiruko|lt_ja=蛭子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Itsukushima Shrine: {{ill|Ichikishima-hime Shrine|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt_ja=市杵嶌姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}

== Events ==
* {{ill|Annual Festival|ja|例祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Annual Festival|1=reisai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385469}} – May 3 to 5 (the mikoshi procession takes place on May 5 every other year)
* New Year’s Festival – December 31

== Access ==
* {{ill|Hankyu Kyoto Main Line|en|Hankyu Kyoto Main Line|WD=Q2053737|lt=Hankyu Kyoto Line}} {{ill|Ōyamazaki Station|en|Ōyamazaki Station|WD=Q3971407|lt=Ōyamazaki Station}} – 40-minute walk
* {{ill|JR Kyōto Line|en|JR Kyōto Line|WD=Q1195820|lt=JR Kyoto Line}} {{ill|Yamazaki Station (Kyoto)|en|Yamazaki Station (Kyoto)|WD=Q1037335|lt=Yamazaki Station}} – 40-minute walk

== What's nearby ==
* Ruins of the {{ill|Battle of Yamazaki|en|Battle of Yamazaki|WD=Q39040|lt=Battle of Yamazaki}}
* Tomb of the {{ill|Seventeen Martyrs|ja|十七烈士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seventeen Martyrs|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Rikyū Hachiman Shrine|ja|離宮八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rikyū Hachiman Shrine|1=Rikyū Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659484}} (Aburaza)
* {{ill|Myōki-an|ja|妙喜庵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōki-an|1=Myōki-an|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11446865}}
** {{ill|Taian|ja|待庵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taian|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 妙喜庵|1=Myōki-an|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11446865}}
* {{ill|Ōyamazaki Sansō Art Museum|ja|大山崎山荘美術館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazaki Sansō Art Museum|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アサヒグループ大山崎山荘美術館|1=Asahi Group Oyamazaki Villa Museum of Art|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11283175}}
* {{ill|Ōyamazaki Town Historical Museum|ja|大山崎町歴史資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazaki Town Historical Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Dainen-ji Temple|ja|大念寺 (京都府大山崎町)|lt=Dainen-ji|lt_ja=大念寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainen-ji Temple|1=Dainen-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435456}}
* {{ill|Yamazaki Seitten|ja|山崎聖天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamazaki Seitten|1=Yamazaki Shōten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11468198}}
* {{ill|Hōshaku-ji|ja|宝積寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōshaku-ji|1=Hōshaku-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452661}}
* {{ill|Ōyamazaki Tile Kiln Site|en|Ōyamazaki Tile Kiln Site|WD=Q109597663|lt=Ōyamazaki Tile Kiln Ruins}} (National {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan}})
* {{ill|Suntory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suntory|qq=}} {{ill|Yamazaki distillery|en|Yamazaki distillery|WD=Q1417541|lt=Yamazaki Distillery}}

== See Also ==
* {{ill|Umenomiya Taisha|en|Umenomiya Taisha|WD=Q276781|lt=Umenomiya Shrine}} – A shrine in Kyoto’s Ukyo Ward enshrining the same deity, Sakatoke, and is sometimes considered to be related.

[[File:Sakatokejinja04 1024.jpg|thumb|240px|{{ill|Mikoshi Storehouse|ja|神輿庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi Storehouse|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]]

'''Sakatoke Shrine''' (自玉手祭来酒解神社, たまでよりまつりきたるさかとけじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine}} located in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Otokuni District, Kyoto|en|Otokuni District, Kyoto|WD=Q376858|lt=Otokun District}}, {{ill|Ōyamazaki, Kyoto|en|Ōyamazaki, Kyoto|WD=Q450287|lt=Ōyamazaki Town}}. Its common name is '''Sakatoke Shrine''' (酒解神社, さかとけじんじゃ, さかときじんじゃ). It is situated near the summit of {{ill|Mount Tennōzan|en|Mount Tennōzan|WD=Q10940452|lt=Tennozan}}. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai Shrine|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|lt=Meishindai|lt_ja=名神大|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and in the old {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was designated as a Gōsha.

==Saijin==
It enshrines {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} as its main deity and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo}} as its secondary deity.

==History==
The origins of its founding are unclear; however, a ridge plaque dating to the first year of the {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō}} era ({{ill|717|en|717|WD=Q23532|lt=717 AD}}) indicates that it was established during the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}. It was formerly known as Yamazaki no Mori (山埼杜) and was enshrined at the site of the present {{ill|Rikyū Hachiman Shrine|ja|離宮八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rikyū Hachiman Shrine|1=Rikyū Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659484}}. In the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, the {{ill|Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} recorded the name as “Yamashiro Province, Otokun District, Sakatoke Shrine, formerly Yamazaki no Mori,” listing it among the state-sponsored {{ill|Meishindai Shrine|ja|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meishindai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} and noting that offerings for the monthly and New Rice Festival were entrusted to it.

Subsequently, the enshrinement at Sakatoke Shrine lapsed, and its whereabouts were unknown until the Meiji period. The present Sakatoke Shrine was formerly the Yamazaki Tenno Shrine, which enshrined the deity Tenno Hachijōjin ({{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō}}) that had been venerated near the summit of Tennozan since medieval times. Although Tennozan was originally called Yamazaki-yama, it came to be known as Tennozan in association with this shrine. In June of the 10th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, the Yamazaki Tenno Shrine was recognized as a Shikinai Shrine and renamed Sakatoke Shrine. The current main deity, Ōyamatsumi, was designated at that time.

The shrine’s mikoshi storehouse has been designated as an important cultural property by the nation.

* Honden – Registered Tangible Cultural Property of the nation ({{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}})
* Mikoshi storehouse – Important Cultural Property of the nation; an Itakura structure from the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}
* Mikoshi (2 units) – Works dating from before the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}}

== Subsidiary shrines ==
* Guardian Shrine: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}}
* Palace Master Shrine: {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|en|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|WD=Q643763|lt=Ashinazuchi, Tenazuchi}}

[[File:Sakatokejinja04 1024.jpg|thumb|240px|{{ill|Mikoshi Storehouse|ja|神輿庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi Storehouse|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]]

'''Sakatoke Shrine''' (自玉手祭来酒解神社, たまでよりまつりきたるさかとけじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine}} located in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Otokuni District, Kyoto|en|Otokuni District, Kyoto|WD=Q376858|lt=Otokun District}}, {{ill|Ōyamazaki, Kyoto|en|Ōyamazaki, Kyoto|WD=Q450287|lt=Ōyamazaki Town}}. Its common name is '''Sakatoke Shrine''' (酒解神社, さかとけじんじゃ, さかときじんじゃ). It is situated near the summit of {{ill|Mount Tennōzan|en|Mount Tennōzan|WD=Q10940452|lt=Tennozan}}. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai Shrine|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|lt=Meishindai|lt_ja=名神大|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and in the old {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was designated as a Gōsha.

==Saijin==
It enshrines {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} as its main deity and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo}} as its secondary deity.

==History==
The origins of its founding are unclear; however, a ridge plaque dating to the first year of the {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō}} era ({{ill|717|en|717|WD=Q23532|lt=717 AD}}) indicates that it was established during the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}. It was formerly known as Yamazaki no Mori (山埼杜) and was enshrined at the site of the present {{ill|Rikyū Hachiman Shrine|ja|離宮八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rikyū Hachiman Shrine|1=Rikyū Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659484}}. In the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, the {{ill|Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} recorded the name as “Yamashiro Province, Otokun District, Sakatoke Shrine, formerly Yamazaki no Mori,” listing it among the state-sponsored {{ill|Meishindai Shrine|ja|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meishindai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} and noting that offerings for the monthly and New Rice Festival were entrusted to it.

Subsequently, the enshrinement at Sakatoke Shrine lapsed, and its whereabouts were unknown until the Meiji period. The present Sakatoke Shrine was formerly the Yamazaki Tenno Shrine, which enshrined the deity Tenno Hachijōjin ({{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō}}) that had been venerated near the summit of Tennozan since medieval times. Although Tennozan was originally called Yamazaki-yama, it came to be known as Tennozan in association with this shrine. In June of the 10th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, the Yamazaki Tenno Shrine was recognized as a Shikinai Shrine and renamed Sakatoke Shrine. The current main deity, Ōyamatsumi, was designated at that time.

The shrine’s mikoshi storehouse has been designated as an important cultural property by the nation.

== cultural property ==
* Honden – Registered Tangible Cultural Property of the nation ({{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}})
* Mikoshi storehouse – Important Cultural Property of the nation; an Itakura structure from the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}
* Mikoshi (2 units) – Works dating from before the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}}

== Subsidiary shrines ==
* Guardian Shrine: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}}
* Palace Master Shrine: {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|en|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|WD=Q643763|lt=Ashinazuchi, Tenazuchi}}
* Three Shrines ({{ill|Sanjagu|ja|三社宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanjagu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}):
** {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} 
** {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|en|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi, Great Deity}}
** {{ill|Hiruko Shrine|ja|Hiruko|lt=Hiruko|lt_ja=蛭子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Itsukushima Shrine: {{ill|Ichikishima-hime Shrine|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt_ja=市杵嶌姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}

== Events ==
* {{ill|Annual Festival|ja|例祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Annual Festival|1=reisai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385469}} – May 3 to 5 (the mikoshi procession takes place on May 5 every other year)
* New Year’s Festival – December 31

== Access ==
* {{ill|Hankyu Kyoto Main Line|en|Hankyu Kyoto Main Line|WD=Q2053737|lt=Hankyu Kyoto Line}} {{ill|Ōyamazaki Station|en|Ōyamazaki Station|WD=Q3971407|lt=Ōyamazaki Station}} – 40-minute walk
* {{ill|JR Kyōto Line|en|JR Kyōto Line|WD=Q1195820|lt=JR Kyoto Line}} {{ill|Yamazaki Station (Kyoto)|en|Yamazaki Station (Kyoto)|WD=Q1037335|lt=Yamazaki Station}} – 40-minute walk

* {{ill|Ōyamazaki Sansō Art Museum|ja|大山崎山荘美術館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazaki Sansō Art Museum|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アサヒグループ大山崎山荘美術館|1=Asahi Group Oyamazaki Villa Museum of Art|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11283175}}

* {{ill|Ōyamazaki Tile Kiln Site|en|Ōyamazaki Tile Kiln Site|WD=Q109597663|lt=Ōyamazaki Tile Kiln Ruins}} (National {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan}})
* {{ill|Suntory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suntory|qq=}} {{ill|Yamazaki distillery|en|Yamazaki distillery|WD=Q1417541|lt=Yamazaki Distillery}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sakatokejinja]][[ja:自玉手祭来酒解神社]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sakatokejinja]][[ja:自玉手祭来酒解神社]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sakatokejinja]][[ja:自玉手祭来酒解神社]]
{
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
|Name = Sakatoke Shrine
|Religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}
|Location = Kyoto Prefecture, {{ill|Otokuni District, Kyoto|en|Otokuni District, Kyoto|WD=Q376858|lt=Otokun District|1=Otokuni District, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Ōyamazaki, Kyoto|en|Ōyamazaki, Kyoto|WD=Q450287|lt=Ōyamazaki Town|1=Ōyamazaki, Kyoto}}, Ōyamazaki Tenno 46
|Deity = {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}
|Type = Shikinai Shrine (Meishindai) / Gōsha
|Honden = Gabled-roof structure
|Festival = {{ill|May 4|en|May 4|WD=Q2549|lt=May 4|1=May 4}}
|image = File:Sakatokejinja03n4592.jpg
|Latitude_deg = 34 |Latitude_min = 54 |Latitude_sec = 5.9 |Lat_dir = N
|Longitude_deg = 135 |Longitude_min = 40 |Longitude_sec = 43.5 |Long_dir = E
|Map_country_code = JP
|Map = Japan Kyoto
|location = {{ill|Oyamazaki|WD=Q450287|1=Ōyamazaki, Kyoto}}
|coordinates = {{Coord|34.901639|N|135.67875|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|deity = {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563|1=Ōyamatsumi}}
}}

[[File:Sakatokejinja01n4592.jpg|thumb|240px|{{ill|Sanjagu|ja|三社宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanjagu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]]
[[File:Sakatokejinja04 1024.jpg|thumb|240px|{{ill|Mikoshi Storehouse|ja|神輿庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi Storehouse|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]]

'''Sakatoke Shrine''' (自玉手祭来酒解神社, たまでよりまつりきたるさかとけじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Otokuni District, Kyoto|en|Otokuni District, Kyoto|WD=Q376858|lt=Otokun District|1=Otokuni District, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Ōyamazaki, Kyoto|en|Ōyamazaki, Kyoto|WD=Q450287|lt=Ōyamazaki Town|1=Ōyamazaki, Kyoto}}. Its common name is '''Sakatoke Shrine''' (酒解神社, さかとけじんじゃ, さかときじんじゃ). It is situated near the summit of {{ill|Mount Tennōzan|en|Mount Tennōzan|WD=Q10940452|lt=Tennozan|1=Mount Tennōzan}}. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai Shrine|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|lt=Meishindai|lt_ja=名神大|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and in the old {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was designated as a Gōsha.

==Saijin==
It enshrines {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} as its main deity and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} as its secondary deity.

==History==
The origins of its founding are unclear; however, a ridge plaque dating to the first year of the {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō|1=Yōrō}} era ({{ill|717|en|717|WD=Q23532|lt=717 AD|1=717}}) indicates that it was established during the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}. It was formerly known as Yamazaki no Mori (山埼杜) and was enshrined at the site of the present {{ill|Rikyū Hachiman Shrine|ja|離宮八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rikyū Hachiman Shrine|1=Rikyū Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659484}}. In the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, the {{ill|Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} recorded the name as “Yamashiro Province, Otokun District, Sakatoke Shrine, formerly Yamazaki no Mori,” listing it among the state-sponsored {{ill|Meishindai Shrine|ja|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meishindai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} and noting that offerings for the monthly and New Rice Festival were entrusted to it.

Subsequently, the enshrinement at Sakatoke Shrine lapsed, and its whereabouts were unknown until the Meiji period. The present Sakatoke Shrine was formerly the Yamazaki Tenno Shrine, which enshrined the deity Tenno Hachijōjin ({{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}) that had been venerated near the summit of Tennozan since medieval times. Although Tennozan was originally called Yamazaki-yama, it came to be known as Tennozan in association with this shrine. In June of the 10th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, the Yamazaki Tenno Shrine was recognized as a Shikinai Shrine and renamed Sakatoke Shrine. The current main deity, Ōyamatsumi, was designated at that time.

The shrine’s mikoshi storehouse has been designated as an important cultural property by the nation.

== cultural property ==
* Honden – Registered Tangible Cultural Property of the nation ({{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}})
* Mikoshi storehouse – Important Cultural Property of the nation; an Itakura structure from the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}
* Mikoshi (2 units) – Works dating from before the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}

== Subsidiary shrines ==
* Guardian Shrine: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}
* Palace Master Shrine: {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|en|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|WD=Q643763|lt=Ashinazuchi, Tenazuchi|1=Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi}}
* Three Shrines ({{ill|Sanjagu|ja|三社宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanjagu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}):
** {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} 
** {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|en|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q595520|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi, Great Deity|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}}
** {{ill|Hiruko Shrine|ja|Hiruko|lt=Hiruko|lt_ja=蛭子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Itsukushima Shrine: {{ill|Ichikishima-hime Shrine|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt_ja=市杵嶌姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}

== Events ==
* {{ill|Annual Festival|ja|例祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Annual Festival|1=Reisai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385469}} – May 3 to 5 (the mikoshi procession takes place on May 5 every other year)
* New Year’s Festival – December 31

== Access ==
* {{ill|Hankyu Kyoto Main Line|en|Hankyu Kyoto Main Line|WD=Q2053737|lt=Hankyu Kyoto Line|1=Hankyu Kyoto Main Line}} {{ill|Ōyamazaki Station|en|Ōyamazaki Station|WD=Q3971407|lt=Ōyamazaki Station|1=Ōyamazaki Station}} – 40-minute walk
* {{ill|JR Kyōto Line|en|JR Kyōto Line|WD=Q1195820|lt=JR Kyoto Line|1=JR Kyōto Line}} {{ill|Yamazaki Station (Kyoto)|en|Yamazaki Station (Kyoto)|WD=Q1037335|lt=Yamazaki Station|1=Yamazaki Station (Kyoto)}} – 40-minute walk

== What's nearby ==
* Ruins of the {{ill|Battle of Yamazaki|en|Battle of Yamazaki|WD=Q39040|lt=Battle of Yamazaki|1=Battle of Yamazaki}}
* Tomb of the {{ill|Seventeen Martyrs|ja|十七烈士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seventeen Martyrs|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Rikyū Hachiman Shrine|ja|離宮八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rikyū Hachiman Shrine|1=Rikyū Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659484}} (Aburaza)
* {{ill|Myōki-an|ja|妙喜庵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōki-an|1=Myōki-an Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11446865}}
** {{ill|Taian|ja|待庵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taian|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 妙喜庵|1=Myōki-an Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11446865}}
* {{ill|Ōyamazaki Sansō Art Museum|ja|大山崎山荘美術館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazaki Sansō Art Museum|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アサヒグループ大山崎山荘美術館|1=Asahi Group Oyamazaki Villa Museum of Art|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11283175}}
* {{ill|Ōyamazaki Town Historical Museum|ja|大山崎町歴史資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazaki Town Historical Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Dainen-ji Temple|ja|大念寺 (京都府大山崎町)|lt=Dainen-ji|lt_ja=大念寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainen-ji Temple|1=Dainen-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435456}}
* {{ill|Yamazaki Seitten|ja|山崎聖天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamazaki Seitten|1=Yamazaki Shōten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11468198}}
* {{ill|Hōshaku-ji|ja|宝積寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōshaku-ji|1=Hōshaku-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452661}}
* {{ill|Ōyamazaki Tile Kiln Site|en|Ōyamazaki Tile Kiln Site|WD=Q109597663|lt=Ōyamazaki Tile Kiln Ruins|1=Ōyamazaki Tile Kiln Site}} (National {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}})
* {{ill|Suntory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suntory|qq=}} {{ill|Yamazaki distillery|en|Yamazaki distillery|WD=Q1417541|lt=Yamazaki Distillery|1=Yamazaki distillery}}

== See Also ==
* {{ill|Umenomiya Taisha|en|Umenomiya Taisha|WD=Q276781|lt=Umenomiya Shrine|1=Umenomiya Taisha}} – A shrine in Kyoto’s Ukyo Ward enshrining the same deity, Sakatoke, and is sometimes considered to be related.

* Honden – Registered Tangible Cultural Property of the nation ({{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}})

{
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
|Name = Sakatoke Shrine
|Religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}
|Location = Kyoto Prefecture, {{ill|Otokuni District, Kyoto|en|Otokuni District, Kyoto|WD=Q376858|lt=Otokun District|1=Otokuni District, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Ōyamazaki, Kyoto|en|Ōyamazaki, Kyoto|WD=Q450287|lt=Ōyamazaki Town|1=Ōyamazaki, Kyoto}}, Ōyamazaki Tenno 46
|Deity = {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}
|Type = Shikinai Shrine (Meishindai) / Gōsha

|Longitude_deg = 135 |Longitude_min = 40 |Longitude_sec = 43.5 |Long_dir = E

|deity = {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563|1=Ōyamatsumi}}
}}

[[File:Sakatokejinja01n4592.jpg|thumb|240px|{{ill|Sanjagu|ja|三社宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanjagu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]]
[[File:Sakatokejinja04 1024.jpg|thumb|240px|{{ill|Mikoshi Storehouse|ja|神輿庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi Storehouse|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]]

'''Sakatoke Shrine''' (自玉手祭来酒解神社, たまでよりまつりきたるさかとけじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Otokuni District, Kyoto|en|Otokuni District, Kyoto|WD=Q376858|lt=Otokun District|1=Otokuni District, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Ōyamazaki, Kyoto|en|Ōyamazaki, Kyoto|WD=Q450287|lt=Ōyamazaki Town|1=Ōyamazaki, Kyoto}}. Its common name is '''Sakatoke Shrine''' (酒解神社, さかとけじんじゃ, さかときじんじゃ). It is situated near the summit of {{ill|Mount Tennōzan|en|Mount Tennōzan|WD=Q10940452|lt=Tennozan|1=Mount Tennōzan}}. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai Shrine|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|lt=Meishindai|lt_ja=名神大|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and in the old {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was designated as a Gōsha.

==Saijin==
It enshrines {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} as its main deity and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} as its secondary deity.

==History==
The origins of its founding are unclear; however, a ridge plaque dating to the first year of the {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō|1=Yōrō}} era ({{ill|717|en|717|WD=Q23532|lt=717 AD|1=717}}) indicates that it was established during the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}. It was formerly known as Yamazaki no Mori (山埼杜) and was enshrined at the site of the present {{ill|Rikyū Hachiman Shrine|ja|離宮八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rikyū Hachiman Shrine|1=Rikyū Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659484}}. In the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, the {{ill|Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} recorded the name as “Yamashiro Province, Otokun District, Sakatoke Shrine, formerly Yamazaki no Mori,” listing it among the state-sponsored {{ill|Meishindai Shrine|ja|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meishindai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} and noting that offerings for the monthly and New Rice Festival were entrusted to it.

Subsequently, the enshrinement at Sakatoke Shrine lapsed, and its whereabouts were unknown until the Meiji period. The present Sakatoke Shrine was formerly the Yamazaki Tenno Shrine, which enshrined the deity Tenno Hachijōjin ({{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}) that had been venerated near the summit of Tennozan since medieval times. Although Tennozan was originally called Yamazaki-yama, it came to be known as Tennozan in association with this shrine. In June of the 10th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, the Yamazaki Tenno Shrine was recognized as a Shikinai Shrine and renamed Sakatoke Shrine. The current main deity, Ōyamatsumi, was designated at that time.

The shrine’s mikoshi storehouse has been designated as an important cultural property by the nation.

== cultural property ==
* Honden – Registered Tangible Cultural Property of the nation ({{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}})
* Mikoshi storehouse – Important Cultural Property of the nation; an Itakura structure from the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}
* Mikoshi (2 units) – Works dating from before the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}

== Subsidiary shrines ==
* Guardian Shrine: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}
* Palace Master Shrine: {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|en|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|WD=Q643763|lt=Ashinazuchi, Tenazuchi|1=Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi}}
* Three Shrines ({{ill|Sanjagu|ja|三社宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanjagu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}):
** {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} 
** {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|en|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q595520|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi, Great Deity|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}}
** {{ill|Hiruko Shrine|ja|Hiruko|lt=Hiruko|lt_ja=蛭子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Itsukushima Shrine: {{ill|Ichikishima-hime Shrine|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt_ja=市杵嶌姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}

== Events ==

'''Sakatoke Shrine''' (自玉手祭来酒解神社, たまでよりまつりきたるさかとけじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Otokuni District, Kyoto|en|Otokuni District, Kyoto|WD=Q376858|lt=Otokun District|1=Otokuni District, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Ōyamazaki, Kyoto|en|Ōyamazaki, Kyoto|WD=Q450287|lt=Ōyamazaki Town|1=Ōyamazaki, Kyoto}}. Its common name is '''Sakatoke Shrine''' (酒解神社, さかとけじんじゃ, さかときじんじゃ). It is situated near the summit of {{ill|Mount Tennōzan|en|Mount Tennōzan|WD=Q10940452|lt=Tennozan|1=Mount Tennōzan}}. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai Shrine|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|lt=Meishindai|lt_ja=名神大|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and in the old {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was designated as a Gōsha.

==Saijin==
It enshrines {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} as its main deity and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} as its secondary deity.

==History==
The origins of its founding are unclear; however, a ridge plaque dating to the first year of the {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō|1=Yōrō}} era ({{ill|717|en|717|WD=Q23532|lt=717 AD|1=717}}) indicates that it was established during the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}. It was formerly known as Yamazaki no Mori (山埼杜) and was enshrined at the site of the present {{ill|Rikyū Hachiman Shrine|ja|離宮八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rikyū Hachiman Shrine|1=Rikyū Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659484}}. In the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, the {{ill|Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} recorded the name as “Yamashiro Province, Otokun District, Sakatoke Shrine, formerly Yamazaki no Mori,” listing it among the state-sponsored {{ill|Meishindai Shrine|ja|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meishindai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} and noting that offerings for the monthly and New Rice Festival were entrusted to it.

Subsequently, the enshrinement at Sakatoke Shrine lapsed, and its whereabouts were unknown until the Meiji period. The present Sakatoke Shrine was formerly the Yamazaki Tenno Shrine, which enshrined the deity Tenno Hachijōjin ({{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}) that had been venerated near the summit of Tennozan since medieval times. Although Tennozan was originally called Yamazaki-yama, it came to be known as Tennozan in association with this shrine. In June of the 10th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, the Yamazaki Tenno Shrine was recognized as a Shikinai Shrine and renamed Sakatoke Shrine. The current main deity, Ōyamatsumi, was designated at that time.

The shrine’s mikoshi storehouse has been designated as an important cultural property by the nation.

== cultural property ==
* Honden – Registered Tangible Cultural Property of the nation ({{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}})
* Mikoshi storehouse – Important Cultural Property of the nation; an Itakura structure from the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}
* Mikoshi (2 units) – Works dating from before the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}

== Subsidiary shrines ==
* Guardian Shrine: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}
* Palace Master Shrine: {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|en|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|WD=Q643763|lt=Ashinazuchi, Tenazuchi|1=Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi}}
* Three Shrines ({{ill|Sanjagu|ja|三社宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanjagu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}):
** {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} 
** {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|en|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q595520|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi, Great Deity|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}}
** {{ill|Hiruko Shrine|ja|Hiruko|lt=Hiruko|lt_ja=蛭子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Itsukushima Shrine: {{ill|Ichikishima-hime Shrine|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt_ja=市杵嶌姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}

== Events ==
* {{ill|Annual Festival|ja|例祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Annual Festival|1=Reisai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385469}} – May 3 to 5 (the mikoshi procession takes place on May 5 every other year)
* New Year’s Festival – December 31

'''Sakatoke Shrine''' (自玉手祭来酒解神社, たまでよりまつりきたるさかとけじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Otokuni District, Kyoto|en|Otokuni District, Kyoto|WD=Q376858|lt=Otokun District|1=Otokuni District, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Ōyamazaki, Kyoto|en|Ōyamazaki, Kyoto|WD=Q450287|lt=Ōyamazaki Town|1=Ōyamazaki, Kyoto}}. Its common name is '''Sakatoke Shrine''' (酒解神社, さかとけじんじゃ, さかときじんじゃ). It is situated near the summit of {{ill|Mount Tennōzan|en|Mount Tennōzan|WD=Q10940452|lt=Tennozan|1=Mount Tennōzan}}. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai Shrine|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|lt=Meishindai|lt_ja=名神大|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and in the old {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was designated as a Gōsha.

==Saijin==
It enshrines {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} as its main deity and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} as its secondary deity.

==History==
The origins of its founding are unclear; however, a ridge plaque dating to the first year of the {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō|1=Yōrō}} era ({{ill|717|en|717|WD=Q23532|lt=717 AD|1=717}}) indicates that it was established during the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}. It was formerly known as Yamazaki no Mori (山埼杜) and was enshrined at the site of the present {{ill|Rikyū Hachiman Shrine|ja|離宮八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rikyū Hachiman Shrine|1=Rikyū Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659484}}. In the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, the {{ill|Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} recorded the name as “Yamashiro Province, Otokun District, Sakatoke Shrine, formerly Yamazaki no Mori,” listing it among the state-sponsored {{ill|Meishindai Shrine|ja|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meishindai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} and noting that offerings for the monthly and New Rice Festival were entrusted to it.

Subsequently, the enshrinement at Sakatoke Shrine lapsed, and its whereabouts were unknown until the Meiji period. The present Sakatoke Shrine was formerly the Yamazaki Tenno Shrine, which enshrined the deity Tenno Hachijōjin ({{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}) that had been venerated near the summit of Tennozan since medieval times. Although Tennozan was originally called Yamazaki-yama, it came to be known as Tennozan in association with this shrine. In June of the 10th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, the Yamazaki Tenno Shrine was recognized as a Shikinai Shrine and renamed Sakatoke Shrine. The current main deity, Ōyamatsumi, was designated at that time.

The shrine’s mikoshi storehouse has been designated as an important cultural property by the nation.

{
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
|Name = Sakatoke Shrine
|Religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}
|Location = Kyoto Prefecture, {{ill|Otokuni District, Kyoto|en|Otokuni District, Kyoto|WD=Q376858|lt=Otokun District|1=Otokuni District, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Ōyamazaki, Kyoto|en|Ōyamazaki, Kyoto|WD=Q450287|lt=Ōyamazaki Town|1=Ōyamazaki, Kyoto}}, Ōyamazaki Tenno 46
|Deity = {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}
|Type = Shikinai Shrine (Meishindai) / Gōsha
|Honden = Gabled-roof structure
|Festival = {{ill|May 4|en|May 4|WD=Q2549|lt=May 4|1=May 4}}

|Longitude_deg = 135 |Longitude_min = 40 |Longitude_sec = 43.5 |Long_dir = E
|Map_country_code = JP
|Map = Japan Kyoto
|location = {{ill|Oyamazaki|WD=Q450287|1=Ōyamazaki, Kyoto}}
|coordinates = {{Coord|34.901639|N|135.67875|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|deity = {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563|1=Ōyamatsumi}}
}}

[[File:Sakatokejinja01n4592.jpg|thumb|240px|{{ill|Sanjagu|ja|三社宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanjagu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]]
[[File:Sakatokejinja04 1024.jpg|thumb|240px|{{ill|Mikoshi Storehouse|ja|神輿庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi Storehouse|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]]

'''Sakatoke Shrine''' (自玉手祭来酒解神社, たまでよりまつりきたるさかとけじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Otokuni District, Kyoto|en|Otokuni District, Kyoto|WD=Q376858|lt=Otokun District|1=Otokuni District, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Ōyamazaki, Kyoto|en|Ōyamazaki, Kyoto|WD=Q450287|lt=Ōyamazaki Town|1=Ōyamazaki, Kyoto}}. Its common name is '''Sakatoke Shrine''' (酒解神社, さかとけじんじゃ, さかときじんじゃ). It is situated near the summit of {{ill|Mount Tennōzan|en|Mount Tennōzan|WD=Q10940452|lt=Tennozan|1=Mount Tennōzan}}. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai Shrine|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|lt=Meishindai|lt_ja=名神大|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and in the old {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was designated as a Gōsha.

==Saijin==
It enshrines {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} as its main deity and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} as its secondary deity.

==History==
The origins of its founding are unclear; however, a ridge plaque dating to the first year of the {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō|1=Yōrō}} era ({{ill|717|en|717|WD=Q23532|lt=717 AD|1=717}}) indicates that it was established during the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}. It was formerly known as Yamazaki no Mori (山埼杜) and was enshrined at the site of the present {{ill|Rikyū Hachiman Shrine|ja|離宮八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rikyū Hachiman Shrine|1=Rikyū Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659484}}. In the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, the {{ill|Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} recorded the name as “Yamashiro Province, Otokun District, Sakatoke Shrine, formerly Yamazaki no Mori,” listing it among the state-sponsored {{ill|Meishindai Shrine|ja|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meishindai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} and noting that offerings for the monthly and New Rice Festival were entrusted to it.

Subsequently, the enshrinement at Sakatoke Shrine lapsed, and its whereabouts were unknown until the Meiji period. The present Sakatoke Shrine was formerly the Yamazaki Tenno Shrine, which enshrined the deity Tenno Hachijōjin ({{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}) that had been venerated near the summit of Tennozan since medieval times. Although Tennozan was originally called Yamazaki-yama, it came to be known as Tennozan in association with this shrine. In June of the 10th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, the Yamazaki Tenno Shrine was recognized as a Shikinai Shrine and renamed Sakatoke Shrine. The current main deity, Ōyamatsumi, was designated at that time.

The shrine’s mikoshi storehouse has been designated as an important cultural property by the nation.

== cultural property ==
* Honden – Registered Tangible Cultural Property of the nation ({{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}})
* Mikoshi storehouse – Important Cultural Property of the nation; an Itakura structure from the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}
* Mikoshi (2 units) – Works dating from before the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}

== Subsidiary shrines ==
* Guardian Shrine: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}
* Palace Master Shrine: {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|en|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|WD=Q643763|lt=Ashinazuchi, Tenazuchi|1=Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi}}
* Three Shrines ({{ill|Sanjagu|ja|三社宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanjagu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}):
** {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} 
** {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|en|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q595520|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi, Great Deity|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}}
** {{ill|Hiruko Shrine|ja|Hiruko|lt=Hiruko|lt_ja=蛭子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Itsukushima Shrine: {{ill|Ichikishima-hime Shrine|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt_ja=市杵嶌姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}

== Events ==
* {{ill|Annual Festival|ja|例祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Annual Festival|1=Reisai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385469}} – May 3 to 5 (the mikoshi procession takes place on May 5 every other year)
* New Year’s Festival – December 31

== Access ==
* {{ill|Hankyu Kyoto Main Line|en|Hankyu Kyoto Main Line|WD=Q2053737|lt=Hankyu Kyoto Line|1=Hankyu Kyoto Main Line}} {{ill|Ōyamazaki Station|en|Ōyamazaki Station|WD=Q3971407|lt=Ōyamazaki Station|1=Ōyamazaki Station}} – 40-minute walk
* {{ill|JR Kyōto Line|en|JR Kyōto Line|WD=Q1195820|lt=JR Kyoto Line|1=JR Kyōto Line}} {{ill|Yamazaki Station (Kyoto)|en|Yamazaki Station (Kyoto)|WD=Q1037335|lt=Yamazaki Station|1=Yamazaki Station (Kyoto)}} – 40-minute walk

== What's nearby ==
* Ruins of the {{ill|Battle of Yamazaki|en|Battle of Yamazaki|WD=Q39040|lt=Battle of Yamazaki|1=Battle of Yamazaki}}
* Tomb of the {{ill|Seventeen Martyrs|ja|十七烈士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seventeen Martyrs|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Rikyū Hachiman Shrine|ja|離宮八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rikyū Hachiman Shrine|1=Rikyū Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659484}} (Aburaza)
* {{ill|Myōki-an|ja|妙喜庵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōki-an|1=Myōki-an Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11446865}}
** {{ill|Taian|ja|待庵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taian|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 妙喜庵|1=Myōki-an Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11446865}}
* {{ill|Ōyamazaki Sansō Art Museum|ja|大山崎山荘美術館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazaki Sansō Art Museum|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アサヒグループ大山崎山荘美術館|1=Asahi Group Oyamazaki Villa Museum of Art|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11283175}}
* {{ill|Ōyamazaki Town Historical Museum|ja|大山崎町歴史資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazaki Town Historical Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Dainen-ji Temple|ja|大念寺 (京都府大山崎町)|lt=Dainen-ji|lt_ja=大念寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainen-ji Temple|1=Dainen-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11435456}}
* {{ill|Yamazaki Seitten|ja|山崎聖天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamazaki Seitten|1=Yamazaki Shōten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11468198}}
* {{ill|Hōshaku-ji|ja|宝積寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōshaku-ji|1=Hōshaku-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452661}}
* {{ill|Ōyamazaki Tile Kiln Site|en|Ōyamazaki Tile Kiln Site|WD=Q109597663|lt=Ōyamazaki Tile Kiln Ruins|1=Ōyamazaki Tile Kiln Site}} (National {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}})
* {{ill|Suntory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suntory|qq=}} {{ill|Yamazaki distillery|en|Yamazaki distillery|WD=Q1417541|lt=Yamazaki Distillery|1=Yamazaki distillery}}

== See Also ==
* {{ill|Umenomiya Taisha|en|Umenomiya Taisha|WD=Q276781|lt=Umenomiya Shrine|1=Umenomiya Taisha}} – A shrine in Kyoto’s Ukyo Ward enshrining the same deity, Sakatoke, and is sometimes considered to be related.

* Honden – Registered Tangible Cultural Property of the nation ({{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}})

== Events ==
* {{ill|Annual Festival|ja|例祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Annual Festival|1=Reisai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385469}} – May 3 to 5 (the mikoshi procession takes place on May 5 every other year)
* New Year’s Festival – December 31
